title: True to Your Deepest Desires (Talk and Short Guided Meditation)
speaker: Rob Burbea
podcast_description: Rob Burbea speaks about jhāna practice through the lens of desire, comparison, and self-view, asking what actually supports practice over time. He argues that the most important work may be learning how to relate to longing, frustration, and difficulty without turning them into a verdict about the self. The talk closes with a short guided meditation that turns toward the beauty of desire, patience, and the one who keeps showing up.
short_summary: A talk on jhāna practice, desire, and self-view, ending with a brief guided meditation on honoring the heart’s longing.

chapters.title: Opening the Question
chapters.description: He sets up the retreat context and the practical problem of questions, comparison, and psychological strain in a mixed group.

chapters.title: Culture and Self-View
chapters.description: He widens the lens to cultural conditioning, the modern sense of self, and why practice can feel so charged now.

chapters.title: You Can Do This
chapters.description: He reminds listeners that practice is possible, that dips do not mean failure, and that responsiveness matters more than panic.

chapters.title: View of Practice
chapters.description: He emphasizes how the self is constructed around practice, the need for a larger view, and the value of showing up without overpressure.

chapters.title: Radical Intention
chapters.description: He introduces exchanging self and other and then turns to the Four Bases of Power, reframing desire as part of liberation rather than a problem to hide.

chapters.title: Desire and Frustration
chapters.description: He explores desire in detail, arguing that what we learn about wanting, goals, and frustration may matter more than any particular jhāna result.

chapters.title: Sorting the Signals
chapters.description: He unpacks motives behind retreat practice and calls for a structural sense of what teachings matter most, not just the most detailed tips.

chapters.title: A Life Shaped by Wanting
chapters.description: Using his own life as a musician, he shows how deep desire can coexist with pain, humility, and long apprenticeship.

chapters.title: Desire Through Dukkha
chapters.description: He distinguishes deep longing from negative self-view, then frames desire as a burning coal that must be related to skillfully.

title: Metta Practice, and a few things about Piti (Instructions)
speaker: Rob Burbea
podcast_description: Rob Burbea offers a practical talk on using metta as a springboard into jhana, while clarifying how that purpose differs from cultivating metta for its own sake. He then turns to piti: what it is, how it can arise in different ways, and how to work with it once it is present through spread, narrow probing, and open receiving. The talk stays close to practice, with repeated emphasis on sensitivity, attunement, enjoyment, and not forcing the process.
short_summary: A practical instruction talk on metta, piti, and the kinds of attention that help them develop into absorption.

chapters.title: Opening the frame
chapters.description: He introduces the talk in two parts and sets up the difference between practice instructions and a broader discussion of view and effort.

chapters.title: Metta as springboard
chapters.description: He distinguishes metta practiced for love from metta used to support jhana, emphasizing intention, sensitivity, and the shift toward piti or well-being.

chapters.title: Attunement and phrases
chapters.description: He expands on how to use phrases, volume, spacing, and bodily responsiveness so the practice stays alive and responsive rather than mechanical.

chapters.title: From metta to joy
chapters.description: He explains how metta can deepen into piti, and why the practitioner may need to discriminate between loving feeling and the pleasure of well-being.

chapters.title: What piti is
chapters.description: He gives a brief definition of piti as non-sensual pleasure or rapture, with examples ranging from tingling and warmth to lightness and vibration.

chapters.title: How piti arises
chapters.description: He surveys several conditions that can give rise to piti, from concentration and energy-body work to insight, open-heartedness, and even nature appreciation.

chapters.title: Let it pervade
chapters.description: Using the Buddha's language, he suggests that once piti appears, the practice is to see whether it can saturate the whole body.

chapters.title: Two modes of attention
chapters.description: He describes two complementary ways of attending to piti: narrow, penetrating attention and open, receptive surrender, moving back and forth between them.

chapters.title: Practice at the edge
chapters.description: He closes the first part by reassuring listeners that the work can be adapted to where they are, even if piti only appears locally or briefly.

title: Breathing with the Energy Body (Guided Meditation)
speaker: Rob Burbea
podcast_description: This guided meditation explores the whole body as an energy body, using posture, breath, and imagination as mutually shaping supports for samadhi. Rob Burbea moves slowly through different points in the body—navel, solar plexus, heart, throat, crown, neck, back, and sacrum—while emphasizing sensitivity to tone, vibration, and what feels helpful. The talk closes with a short teaching section on how to hold these methods lightly and keep a clear sense of what is primary and what is secondary in practice.
short_summary: A guided exploration of the energy body in which breath, posture, and imagination are used to cultivate whole-body presence and ease.

chapters.title: Settling the posture
chapters.description: The meditation begins by adjusting posture, sensing contact and uprightness, and noticing how posture and mind shape one another.

chapters.title: Whole-body awareness
chapters.description: Attention is widened over the whole body space and the invitation is to notice texture, vibration, and the energy of the body.

chapters.title: Long breath and expansion
chapters.description: The breath is encouraged to become long, slow, and comfortable while the whole energy body is felt as expanding, contracting, and being energized.

chapters.title: Breath at the navel
chapters.description: The practice shifts into a tour of imagined breath entering near the navel, using the image to notice how different breaths affect the whole field.

chapters.title: Moving through the midline
chapters.description: The breath point is moved to the right side, then the midline below the navel and up toward the solar plexus, with attention to what feels right.

chapters.title: Heart and throat tour
chapters.description: The tour continues upward through the heart, then the front of the throat, exploring ripples, currents, and the effect on the whole body space.

chapters.title: Crown, neck, and back
chapters.description: The breath is imagined at the crown, then at the base of the neck and the back of the heart, with emphasis on imagination, kinesthetic feeling, and responsiveness.

chapters.title: Sacrum and release
chapters.description: The tour reaches the sacrum and tailbone, then returns to whole-space awareness and lets the breath settle into whatever it wants to do.

chapters.title: Practice reflections
chapters.description: After the meditation, Burbea explains that there is no fixed order, that the energy body practice offers many possibilities, and that other supports like mettā can be used as secondary ingredients when helpful.

title: The Energy Body and the Whole-body breath (Instructions and Guided Meditation)
speaker: Rob Burbea
podcast_description: Rob Burbea begins with a short explanation of why this retreat will focus on whole-body breath and the energy body as supports for samadhi. He then leads a long guided meditation, moving from posture and whole-body awareness into the felt qualities of breath, including expansion, energization, relaxation, and subtle energy currents. In the closing talk, he returns to the same theme in practical terms: experiment rather than default, and let attention become lighter, more responsive, and more subtle both on and off the cushion.
short_summary: A guided exploration of whole-body breath, subtle energy, and the kind of responsive attention that supports samadhi.

chapters.title: Opening the approach
chapters.description: He explains the retreat context and sets up the session as a guided meditation followed by teaching.

chapters.title: Whole body and energy
chapters.description: He defines what he means by whole body and energy body, distinguishing subtle felt texture from ordinary bodily sensation.

chapters.title: A teaching story
chapters.description: He recalls being taught this approach and then being asked, in a Mahasi context, what he was actually attending to.

chapters.title: Posture and presence
chapters.description: The guided meditation begins with careful posture, then expands awareness into the whole body as a living field.

chapters.title: Breath in the whole body
chapters.description: He invites the longest comfortable breath and asks listeners to feel expansion, contraction, energization, and relaxation throughout the whole space.

chapters.title: Solar plexus play
chapters.description: The practice opens to imagination and subtle currents around the solar plexus, with emphasis on experimentation rather than certainty.

chapters.title: What feels best
chapters.description: He encourages trying different breath lengths and textures to notice which supports the most ease, clarity, and pleasure in the body.

chapters.title: Review and principle
chapters.description: After the meditation, he recaps the main instructions and reframes samadhi as a path of refinement, subtlety, and responsiveness.

chapters.title: Daily life practice
chapters.description: He closes by extending whole-body awareness into ordinary activity and linking it with appreciation, nourishment, and mudita.

title: Counting Within the Breath (Guided Meditation)
speaker: Rob Burbea
podcast_description: Rob Burbea offers a guided meditation built around counting within the breath, moving through a longer count, then a shorter one, while keeping whole-body awareness open. After the practice, he explains why this method can steady attention, counter inertia, and support the conditions for jhana. He also gives practical retreat guidance on posture, movement, interviews, and how to work with comfort and energy.
short_summary: A guided practice using internal breath-counting, followed by an explanation of how it supports steadiness, energy, and jhana practice.

chapters.title: Counting Within Breath
chapters.description: Rob introduces the uncommon method of counting inside each breath and guides the group through the practice with whole-body awareness.

chapters.title: Settling After Practice
chapters.description: He checks in on how the exercise felt and notes that it may be awkward or helpful in different ways for different people.

chapters.title: Why It Helps
chapters.description: Rob explains that the practice can counter low energy and provide more support for attention, especially at the start of retreat.

chapters.title: Attention and Jhana
chapters.description: He describes how the practice can be developed and says it may bring the mind close to first jhana if it is carefully cultivated.

chapters.title: Posture and Comfort
chapters.description: Rob emphasizes that an upright, comfortable posture matters and points people to a posture session for further help.

chapters.title: Movement and Energy
chapters.description: He recommends balancing sitting with walking, standing, gentle movement, and time outside to support the body and the retreat energy.

chapters.title: Interviews and Practice Focus
chapters.description: Rob explains how interviews will work and asks participants to bring questions mainly about their meditation practice and jhana work.

chapters.title: Practical Questions
chapters.description: He closes with guidance on pace, room use, water, chairs, standing meditation, and other retreat logistics before ending for the evening.

title: Intention and Devotion (Guided Meditation)
speaker: Rob Burbea
description: (Gaia House) Please Note: This series of talks is from a retreat led by Rob Burbea and Catherine McGee for experienced practitioners. The requirements for participation included some understanding of and working familiarity with practices of emptiness, samatha, metta, the emotional/energy body, and the imaginal, as well as basic mindfulness practice. Without this experience it is possible that the material and teachings from this retreat will be difficult to understand and confusing for some.
podcast_description: Rob Burbea explores intention and devotion as ways of opening practice toward sacredness, moving from a contrast with renunciation into the more complex, generative terrain of imaginal practice. He then leads a guided exercise that uses standing, walking, and stillness to sense the body, heart, and energy body as part of a lived orientation toward the sacred. The talk closes with a quiet emphasis on humility, trust, and the need to keep returning to intention gently, like tending a flame.
short_summary: A guided meditation on intention as devotion to discovering sacredness in body, mind, and world.

chapters.title: Renunciation and desire
chapters.description: Burbea begins by contrasting simplifying practices with the more generative movement of opening to desire and imaginal practice.

chapters.title: Imaginal complexity
chapters.description: He describes imaginal practice as erotic, proliferating, and complexifying, with images generating deeper dimensions and sacredness.

chapters.title: The body becomes sacred
chapters.description: The talk turns to how the body can be re-imagined as object, subject, and locus of knowing, not just something functional or culturally defined.

chapters.title: Movement and stillness
chapters.description: Burbea opens meditation beyond strict stillness, allowing walking, gentle motion, or inward movement in stillness if it feels soulful.

chapters.title: Intention toward sacredness
chapters.description: He names intention as the wish to discover more sacredness in practice and in life, and invites the group into a shared exercise.

chapters.title: Standing exercise
chapters.description: Participants are guided to feel rootedness, heart, and energy body, then to sense longing and devotion as an orientation toward sacredness.

chapters.title: Walking with holy fire
chapters.description: The exercise expands into open-sense walking, with the intention to let all that is perceived be seen as sacred, including others and the room itself.

chapters.title: Returning to the flame
chapters.description: After a period of silence, he speaks about intention as impermanent and the need to return gently, maintaining care for body, heart, and soul.

chapters.title: Bowing and closing
chapters.description: The talk ends with bowing to the sacredness one wants to know, and a final reminder not to lose that care while rearranging the room.

title: Eros in the Imaginal (Guided Meditation)
speaker: Rob Burbea
description: (Gaia House) Please Note: This series of talks is from a retreat led by Rob Burbea and Catherine McGee for experienced practitioners. The requirements for participation included some understanding of and working familiarity with practices of emptiness, samatha, metta, the emotional/energy body, and the imaginal, as well as basic mindfulness practice. Without this experience it is possible that the material and teachings from this retreat will be difficult to understand and confusing for some.
podcast_description: Rob Burbea begins by clarifying what he means by "imaginal": not merely visualization, but a way of perceiving that can involve the body, relationship, the emotional field, and a sense of mystery or sacredness. He then leads a long guided meditation that moves from settling the energy body into inviting an image, exploring how an imaginal figure relates, gazes, resonates, and sometimes becomes sexual or transfigures bodily assumptions. The talk ends by stepping back to explain how images may be discovered or deliberately chosen, and how energy body, emptiness, and ordinary life can all become entrances into imaginal practice.
short_summary: A teaching and guided meditation on how imaginal practice and eros emerge through the energy body, relationship, and soul resonance.

chapters.title: What Imaginal Means
chapters.description: Burbea defines imaginal practice as more than visualization, more than something purely internal, and more than something only visual.

chapters.title: Settling Into Practice
chapters.description: He invites the group into posture and stillness before opening attention to the body and the energy field.

chapters.title: Energy Body and Samadhi
chapters.description: The meditation begins with sensing the energy body, tending well-being, and using a light, caring mode of attention.

chapters.title: Letting An Image Form
chapters.description: An image is invited to arise, either from looseness, difficulty, or deliberate choice, and to be met in relationship.

chapters.title: Ways Of Relating
chapters.description: He explores several modes of contact with the image: as a dyad, by identifying with it, by witnessing it, or by noticing mutual gaze and exchange.

chapters.title: Eros And Transformation
chapters.description: The practice opens into being with, moving with, and potentially sexual contact, while staying rooted in sensitivity and non-hierarchy.

chapters.title: Primary Sense And Resonance
chapters.description: Attention turns to the image's dominant sense modality, its element, solidity, and the emotional and bodily resonance it evokes.

chapters.title: Choosing And Receiving Images
chapters.description: He closes by explaining that images may arrive or be chosen, and that practice can begin from dream images, people, the room, emotions, emptiness, or the energy body.

title: Images of the Breath: Energy Body, Colour, and the Beloved (Guided Meditation)
speaker: Rob Burbea
description: (Gaia House) Please Note: This series of talks is from a retreat led by Rob Burbea and Catherine McGee for experienced practitioners. The requirements for participation included some understanding of and working familiarity with practices of emptiness, samatha, metta, the emotional/energy body, and the imaginal, as well as basic mindfulness practice. Without this experience it is possible that the material and teachings from this retreat will be difficult to understand and confusing for some.
podcast_description: Rob Burbea offers a guided meditation that moves from the felt energy of the body into several imaginal ways of breathing: through different centers, through the whole field, as light, and as color. The practice then opens into blessing and relationship, including the possibility of meeting an imaginal beloved and breathing in harmony with that figure. The talk ends by inviting listeners to notice how image changes the sense of self, others, and world.
short_summary: A guided meditation on breath, energy body, light, color, and imaginal relationship.

chapters.title: Why images matter
chapters.description: Burbea briefly frames imaginal practice as something that develops with familiarity and interest, and notes how it can interweave with samadhi and emptiness practice.

chapters.title: Settling the body
chapters.description: The guided meditation begins by establishing posture, spreading awareness through the body, and sensing the energy field as alive and responsive.

chapters.title: Lower centers and grounding
chapters.description: He explores breathing at the lower belly, perineum, feet, and lower back, inviting attention to what feels comfortable and good.

chapters.title: Heart and crown
chapters.description: The practice expands to the back of the heart and then the crown of the head, always emphasizing that the whole body remains involved.

chapters.title: Front of body opening
chapters.description: Attention moves to the chest and solar plexus, with the breath imagined as opening and softening the body from the front.

chapters.title: Porous field of breath
chapters.description: Burbea invites the sense of breath and energy arriving from all sides or growing from within, and lets the breath become very subtle or still.

chapters.title: Light and color
chapters.description: The breath is then felt as light, given color, and noticed for its different qualities, with green, red, and blue used as examples of distinct felt tones.

chapters.title: Blessing radiance
chapters.description: The light is allowed to radiate outward as healing or blessing, reshaping the surrounding world into a sacred mandala or Buddha realm.

chapters.title: Meeting the beloved
chapters.description: An imaginal figure, perhaps a beloved, is brought into the field and the meditation explores breathing together, passing breath back and forth, and opening to love.

title: Guided Meditation 1
speaker: Rob Burbea
podcast_description: This guided meditation begins with settling posture and releasing obvious tension in the face, throat, shoulders, chest, and belly. It then widens into a larger field of awareness, first as a spacious bodily sense and then as a bright, energetic space that can be supported by imagined lines of energy and, optionally, the breath. The practice gradually becomes more imaginal, suggesting a radiant body of light and inviting the listener to notice what feels helpful rather than forcing any one visualization.
short_summary: A sparse guided meditation that moves from posture and relaxation into spacious, luminous, imaginal awareness.

chapters.title: Settling the posture
chapters.description: The meditation begins by establishing an upright but relaxed posture and sensing a balance of wakefulness and softness.

chapters.title: Releasing tension
chapters.description: Attention moves through the face, throat, neck, shoulders, back, chest, and belly, encouraging gentle relaxation and release.

chapters.title: Grounding in contact
chapters.description: The practice widens to simple sensations in the hands, backside, legs, and feet, grounding awareness in immediate bodily contact.

chapters.title: Opening the field
chapters.description: Awareness is invited to expand beyond the body into a larger space filled with bright, sensitive presence.

chapters.title: Restoring spacious presence
chapters.description: The talk emphasizes repeatedly re-expanding attention when it shrinks, using a wind-and-sail image to support an open, filled space.

chapters.title: Imagining energy lines
chapters.description: A central line of energy is introduced, then extended into additional lines down the legs, with an emphasis on sensing what feels supportive.

chapters.title: Breath and support
chapters.description: The meditation offers the breath as an optional support for the whole-body field, including gentle expansion on the in-breath and relaxation on the out-breath.

chapters.title: Experimenting with form
chapters.description: Listeners are encouraged to try different combinations of breath, energy lines, and imagination, always returning to the felt sense of the whole space.

chapters.title: Body of light
chapters.description: The practice deepens into a radiant or translucent body of light, with color and luminosity left open to the listener's own response.

title: Body/Image - A Guided Meditation
speaker: Rob Burbea
podcast_description: Rob Burbea offers a guided meditation that begins with grounding in the felt sense of the body and a gentle frame of kindness. The practice then moves through a detailed contemplative exploration of body parts and elements, shifting from anatomy into imagination, elemental reflection, and questions about solidity, space, and perception. In the final section, he introduces a luminous mind-made body as an optional imaginal exercise, then returns to stillness and integration.
short_summary: A guided exploration of the body that moves from anatomy and elemental contemplation into imaginal freedom and back to grounded stillness.

chapters.title: Settling In
chapters.description: The meditation begins by finding posture, feeling the body from within, and orienting toward a simple, kind way of practicing.

chapters.title: Practice as Kindness
chapters.description: He frames the whole exercise as a gift of kindness to oneself and suggests holding the meditation inside that perspective.

chapters.title: Exploring the Lower Body
chapters.description: Attention moves through the legs and feet with anatomical detail, while staying lightly connected to the whole body and the heart's response.

chapters.title: Pelvis and Digestive System
chapters.description: The exploration continues through the genitals, bladder, kidneys, and digestive organs, emphasizing playfulness and a sense of wonder.

chapters.title: Head and Nervous System
chapters.description: He turns to the head, eyes, teeth, skull, brain, spinal cord, and nerves, inviting a fuller sense of the body's intricate sensitivity.

chapters.title: Elements and Interbeing
chapters.description: Water, air, fire, and earth are contemplated as shared elements that blur the boundary between body and world.

chapters.title: Solidity and Mystery
chapters.description: The talk moves into atoms, space, and the appearance of matter, opening into non-thingness and the role of consciousness in perception.

chapters.title: Luminous Body
chapters.description: An optional imaginal practice invites the listener to draw out a radiant body of light, float and move freely, then return it to the physical body.

chapters.title: Integration
chapters.description: The meditation closes by re-grounding in the body, stillness, awareness, and a final return to kindness and care.

title: Working with the Emotional Body (Instructions and Guided Meditation: Day Seven)
speaker: Rob Burbea
description: (Gaia House) This series of guided meditations and instructions presents a set of tools and approaches for working skilfully with emotions and mind states in practice.
podcast_description: Rob Burbea offers a reflective wrap-up on working with emotions as one angle among many, emphasizing that there is no single best way to meet emotional life. He moves between seeing emotions as body sensations, as stories, as inner characters, and as fabrications shaped by self-view, while also noting that these views can be useful at different times. The talk ends by widening the frame: emotions can be healthy signals, traces of the past, or expressions of the psyche, and practice asks what each perspective opens up.
short_summary: A quiet, wide-ranging reflection on different ways of understanding emotions, self-view, and the making of experience in meditation.

chapters.title: One Perspective Among Many
chapters.description: Burbea frames the talk as a tying-together of previous instructions rather than a new practice.

chapters.title: Inner Dynamics and Characters
chapters.description: He explores emotions as dynamic forces or as inner persons, especially the useful and risky ways of working with an inner critic.

chapters.title: Stories and Simplicity
chapters.description: The talk turns to how stories can either simplify experience through direct sensation or harden it into a fixed self-view.

chapters.title: How Emotion Forms
chapters.description: He describes seeing emotion arise in quiet meditation and suggests that emotions may be habitually fabricated rather than simply stored and released.

chapters.title: What Fabrication Gives
chapters.description: Burbea asks what an emotion may be giving us, including not only suffering but sometimes richness, soulfulness, or depth.

chapters.title: Self-View and Looping
chapters.description: He examines the loop between self-view and emotion, illustrated by the practice of exposing a self-blaming story until it becomes visible and absurd.

chapters.title: Courage to Feel
chapters.description: The talk widens to courage on both sides: to feel what is difficult, and also to let feeling quiet down when that threatens identity.

chapters.title: Resources Becoming Accessible
chapters.description: Burbea closes by distinguishing cathartic release from releasing strength or power into accessibility, then gathers the many perspectives into a final synthesis.

title: Working With The Emotional Body (Instructions and Guided Meditation: Day Six)
speaker: Rob Burbea
description: (Gaia House) This series of guided meditations and instructions presents a set of tools and approaches for working skilfully with emotions and mind states in practice.
podcast_description: This guided meditation and teaching explores emotional practice through the simple move of allowing experience to be exactly as it is. Rob Burbea begins with posture, care, and a strong emphasis on kindness, then develops the idea that aversion and grasping add much of the suffering we feel. He goes on to connect this with anger, fear, the second arrow, and a deeper reflection on how relationship helps shape what we experience.
short_summary: A guided meditation on allowing experience, with teaching on aversion, emotional pain, and the second arrow.

chapters.title: Settling and kindness
chapters.description: The talk opens as a guided sitting, grounding in posture, balance, and a sense of practice as kindness rather than self-measurement.

chapters.title: Meeting what is prominent
chapters.description: Attention turns to whatever is most present in the body or mind, with instructions to draw close and know it intimately.

chapters.title: Allowing as practice
chapters.description: Burbea develops allowing as full welcoming and softening, repeating it as the central gesture of the meditation.

chapters.title: Softening and letting go
chapters.description: The instructions continue through welcoming, melting, and letting go, with a strong emphasis on presence over precision.

chapters.title: Anger and fear
chapters.description: He pauses for questions and then explains how anger and fear are sustained by aversion, contraction, and a wish to push experience away.

chapters.title: The second arrow
chapters.description: The teaching returns to the frame of suffering and reactivity, using the second-arrow image to show how allowing eases added pain.

chapters.title: Relationship shapes experience
chapters.description: Burbea moves into a more subtle point: experience is affected by the way it is met, and he links this to emptiness and the mind's way of looking.

chapters.title: Letting be, not control
chapters.description: He warns against turning allowing into a hidden strategy and offers alternative words like welcoming, embracing, and letting be.

chapters.title: Doership and closing advice
chapters.description: The close touches on allowing even the wish for things to go away, the fading of doership, and a brief practical reminder for the rest of the day.

title: Working with the Emotional Body (Instructions and Guided Meditation: Day Four)
speaker: Rob Burbea
description: (Gaia House) This series of guided meditations and instructions presents a set of tools and approaches for working skilfully with emotions and mind states in practice.
podcast_description: Rob Burbea reviews the previous days of practice and then leads a guided meditation centered on posture, kindness, and widening awareness from the body into sound and space. The talk gradually shifts from intimate contact with experience to a much larger context, where thoughts, sensations, and even contraction can arise and pass without becoming a problem. In the closing discussion, he names fear, stillness, and the Buddha’s salt crystal metaphor to show how the felt weight of experience changes when it is held in a larger field.
short_summary: A guided meditation and teaching on widening awareness so emotions and experience are held in a larger, less entangling context.

chapters.title: Review and orientation
chapters.description: He briefly reviews the earlier exercises and emphasizes that the practices are non-linear and different for different people.

chapters.title: Posture and kindness
chapters.description: The guided meditation begins with uprightness, softness, and an explicit sense of practice as care and loving attention.

chapters.title: Centering the body
chapters.description: Attention returns to the center of the body with a light, curious holding of whatever is most prominent.

chapters.title: Opening to sound
chapters.description: Awareness expands from the body to listening, receiving sounds as a total field rather than one object after another.

chapters.title: Space and letting go
chapters.description: He invites a wide, 360-degree awareness in which sounds, sensations, and thoughts arise and dissolve into silence.

chapters.title: Including contraction
chapters.description: The teaching turns to allowing even contraction and discomfort to belong to the same open space, without making them personal.

chapters.title: Discussion and salt crystal
chapters.description: In discussion he responds to reports of fear and stillness, then uses the Buddha’s salt crystal image to explain how context changes the felt impact of pain.

chapters.title: Choosing the larger context
chapters.description: He answers a question about staying interested in spacious awareness and ends with practical encouragement for walking, nature, and experimenting in daily practice.

title: Working with the Emotional Body (Instructions and Guided Meditation: Day Three)
speaker: Rob Burbea
description: (Gaia House) This series of guided meditations and instructions presents a set of tools and approaches for working skilfully with emotions and mind states in practice.
podcast_description: Rob Burbea offers a compact set of ways to work with emotions in meditation by shifting attention through the body, noticing how labeling and interpretation shape experience, and using perception more intentionally. He moves from reviewing the earlier exercise to practical experiments with the emotional body, the mind's commentary, and simple supportive thoughts. The talk closes by encouraging a lighter, quicker relationship to experience, with kindness kept in view throughout.
short_summary: A guided instruction on working with emotions through body awareness, labeling, assumptions, and deliberately supportive perception.

chapters.title: Reviewing the approach
chapters.description: Burbea reviews the earlier practice of attending along a central axis and orienting toward bodily and emotional experience.

chapters.title: The energy body
chapters.description: He broadens the practice into a larger field or energy body and explains why such images can be useful without being taken literally.

chapters.title: Feeling and enjoyment
chapters.description: The instruction turns toward sensing the texture of the whole space, including permission to enjoy pleasant feeling when it is present.

chapters.title: Mind's commentary
chapters.description: He asks practitioners to notice judgments, assumptions, and conclusions the mind adds to experience, including how they shape self-view.

chapters.title: Short practice experiments
chapters.description: A set of brief exercises invites direct checking-in, noticing commentary, and seeing what happens when attention is refreshed quickly.

chapters.title: A supportive view
chapters.description: Burbea suggests experimenting with the thought that this moment is exactly right, and explores how views affect experience.

chapters.title: Unweighting the moment
chapters.description: He introduces the idea of shortening the felt span of past and future so attention can outweigh the emotion's pull.

chapters.title: Quickness and kindness
chapters.description: The talk ends by reframing distraction as normal, encouraging short check-ins, and returning to a climate of kindness.

title: Working with the Emotional Body (Instructions and Guided Meditation: Day Two)
speaker: Rob Burbea
description: (Gaia House) This series of guided meditations and instructions presents a set of tools and approaches for working skilfully with emotions and mind states in practice.
podcast_description: Rob Burbea offers a set of practical instructions for working with tiredness, physical discomfort, and emotional difficulty in meditation. He emphasizes a patient, exploratory attention: softening aversion, broadening awareness, and noticing how experience changes moment by moment. The talk includes a guided practice and short reflections on how a more open field of attention can become a source of safety and resource.
short_summary: A guided Dharma talk on meeting difficult experience with curiosity, softness, and a wider field of awareness.

chapters.title: Introducing the approach
chapters.description: Burbea frames the practice as non-linear and invites patience with the gradual unfolding of skills.

chapters.title: Tiredness and aversion
chapters.description: He explores tiredness as a mix of bodily sensation and resistance, and suggests looking for the smaller experience beneath the sense of overwhelm.

chapters.title: Working with discomfort
chapters.description: He turns to itch and pain, recommending relaxation, broader awareness, and a gentler relationship to discomfort.

chapters.title: Softness and detail
chapters.description: Burbea contrasts hard, laser-like attention with a lighter touch, then shows how even calm or space can be noticed and cultivated.

chapters.title: Cézanne and patience
chapters.description: A passage on Cézanne supports the teaching that sustained, patient looking can make experience vivid and alive.

chapters.title: Guided central line practice
chapters.description: He begins the meditation, asking listeners to settle, touch the center line, and stay with whatever is most prominent without pressure.

chapters.title: Expanding the field
chapters.description: The practice widens from the center line into a balloon-like space of awareness that includes the whole body.

chapters.title: Inquiry and resourcing
chapters.description: Burbea responds to participants' comments, clarifying the difference between detailed attention and the more diffuse, protective field of awareness.

chapters.title: Two practices to explore
chapters.description: He closes by distinguishing the central-line and bubble practices, and encourages playful experimentation with both during walking and sitting.

title: Working with the Emotional Body (Instructions and Guided Meditation: Day One)
speaker: Rob Burbea
description: (Gaia House) This series of guided meditations and instructions presents a set of tools and approaches for working skilfully with emotions and mind states in practice.
podcast_description: Rob Burbea offers an introduction to working with emotions and mind states through the body, framing practice around the Pali term citta as heart-mind. He emphasizes not trying to get rid of feeling, but learning to meet it with interest, kindness, and enough attention that difficult states become workable rather than overwhelming. The talk moves from explanation into two guided practices, with instructions for sitting and walking meditation and a steady return to the body as the place where emotion can be known.
short_summary: An introduction to meeting emotions through the body, with guided practice on holding feeling in awareness rather than being flooded by it.

chapters.title: Heart-Mind Framing
chapters.description: Burbea introduces citta as heart-mind and sets out the basic questions of what we want from emotional practice.

chapters.title: What We Want
chapters.description: He names the aims of emotional work: fullness, openness, the ability to unknot difficulty, and growing confidence.

chapters.title: Mind States And Emotion
chapters.description: The talk distinguishes emotions from subtler mind states and explains why awareness of both levels matters.

chapters.title: Helpful Attention
chapters.description: He describes how attention and emotion can tip a seesaw, and why the way we attend can either help or worsen a state.

chapters.title: Body-Based Practice
chapters.description: Burbea leads a first guided sitting practice, inviting listeners to feel posture, bodily sensations, and the emotional state expressed in the body.

chapters.title: Allowing And Exploring
chapters.description: The instruction deepens into allowing, drawing close, noticing changing textures, and staying with whatever is present without demanding clarity.

chapters.title: Letting Go Of Demands
chapters.description: He addresses expectations directly, using the image of waves at the seaside to show how shifting experience can build trust.

chapters.title: Second Sitting
chapters.description: A shorter meditation repeats the central themes of ease, kindness, and checking in with the heart-state on the central line of the body.

chapters.title: Object And Walking Practice
chapters.description: The close of the talk clarifies the shift from being inside the emotion to seeing it as an object in awareness, then gives walking meditation instructions.

title: Guided Meditation - The Play of Appearances
speaker: Rob Burbea
podcast_description: This guided meditation begins with settling the posture and letting awareness pervade the body, then moves into a gentle practice of receiving whatever arises. From there, the instruction broadens toward welcoming thoughts, emotions, and self-images as appearances in space, without making them into a problem. The meditation closes by emphasizing release, the felt peace of letting go, and a quiet sense that all things belong to awareness.
short_summary: A guided practice of softening, welcoming experience, and resting in the peace of letting go.

chapters.title: Settling the seat
chapters.description: The meditation opens by finding the seat, establishing posture, and letting awareness spread through the body and mind.

chapters.title: Receiving and softening
chapters.description: Instruction turns toward a tender, receptive awareness that holds experience with softness, warmth, and ease.

chapters.title: Appearances in space
chapters.description: Reactivity is reframed as part of the play of appearances, and the practice widens to include everything that arises.

chapters.title: Welcoming selfing
chapters.description: Thoughts, images, and definitions of self are allowed to appear without being taken as solid or problematic.

chapters.title: Bowing to experience
chapters.description: The talk deepens into allowing and bowing to the mysterious display of empty appearances, with tenderness and love.

chapters.title: Letting the heart release
chapters.description: Repeated invitations to let go point toward a simple release in which sensations, thoughts, and emotions can come and go.

chapters.title: Floating in space
chapters.description: Experience is described as insubstantial and belonging to space, with sensations and sounds held in silence.

chapters.title: Peace in all things
chapters.description: The meditation ends with sustained letting go and the repeated recognition that the ultimate nature of all things is peace.

title: Seventh Instructions and Guided Metta Meditation: Metta and Emptiness
speaker: Rob Burbea
podcast_description: Rob Burbea begins by framing metta as a practice with six flexible categories, then turns to the move from a personal wish for goodwill toward an open, boundless love that can include all beings. He explores how spaciousness, perception, and samadhi change what feels like a problem, and how aversion quietly closes the heart in ordinary situations like tiredness, cold weather, or wanting comfort. The talk ends with practical encouragement to question habitual patterns gently and to keep the practice responsive rather than rigid.
short_summary: A talk on expanding metta from specific categories into an open, spacious, and sometimes impersonal sense of love, while noticing how aversion closes the heart.

chapters.title: Metta categories
chapters.description: He introduces the six metta categories and emphasizes choosing what is most helpful in the moment.

chapters.title: Turning toward self
chapters.description: He speaks about the healing value of self-metta, while warning against turning it into a fearful withholding of love.

chapters.title: Methods for all beings
chapters.description: He describes ways of extending metta by direction or by categories of beings, then offers a simpler approach based on body sense and radiating spaciousness.

chapters.title: Space filled with love
chapters.description: He explains how phrases can be used or dropped, and how spacious awareness can reveal a sense of love woven into experience.

chapters.title: Perception and emptiness
chapters.description: He shifts to the main point that changes in perception matter more than dramatic experiences, and links this directly to emptiness.

chapters.title: Samadhi and insight
chapters.description: He notes that quieter, more settled states give insights a better chance to take root, even when the insight itself feels ordinary.

chapters.title: Aversion closes the heart
chapters.description: Using weather, tiredness, and comfort as examples, he shows how aversion narrows the body and consciousness and how that narrowing is connected to love.

chapters.title: Questioning habit
chapters.description: He explores how closing down can masquerade as care, and returns again and again to the importance of keeping inquiry alive rather than forcing a right answer.

chapters.title: Return to practice
chapters.description: He briefly returns to Catherine McGee's paired-practice framing, then offers a short guided settling and metta closing toward unbounded kindness.

title: Fifth Instructions and Guided Metta Meditation
speaker: Rob Burbea
podcast_description: Rob Burbea continues a metta retreat teaching by introducing the neutral person and exploring why this category can feel dull, spacious, or unexpectedly difficult. He reflects on shared humanity, vulnerability, mortality, and the balance between metta and compassion, then turns into a guided practice that first settles the body and then opens the heart toward a neutral person. The meditation emphasizes gentle persistence, flexible phrases, and letting care spread without forcing it.
short_summary: A teaching and guided metta practice focused on opening the heart to the neutral person, with reflections on shared vulnerability and the balance between metta and compassion.

chapters.title: Introducing the neutral person
chapters.description: Burbea explains how the neutral person enters metta practice and why this category often feels hard to notice at first.

chapters.title: Shared humanity
chapters.description: He develops reflections on common desires, suffering, mortality, and vulnerability as ways of widening the heart.

chapters.title: Phrases and flexibility
chapters.description: He discusses how metta can be carried in ordinary life, and how repeated phrases can stay alive by shifting in meaning.

chapters.title: Stretching the heart
chapters.description: Burbea contrasts the narrative self with the simpler felt sense of a being, and speaks about balancing ease with deliberate stretching.

chapters.title: Self, friend, and shadow
chapters.description: He turns to the difficulty of self-metta, warning against getting stuck in self-focus while also affirming its healing value.

chapters.title: Settling into practice
chapters.description: The guided meditation begins with posture, body awareness, and gently sustained metta phrases that open into the whole body.

chapters.title: Neutral person meditation
chapters.description: He invites the image of a neutral person and offers reflections and phrases that include their difficulties, joys, and wish for peace.

chapters.title: Closing and return
chapters.description: The practice softens into a final sense of warmth, simple pleasures, and a return to the body with care.

title: Fourth Instructions and Guided Metta Meditation
speaker: Rob Burbea
podcast_description: Rob Burbea offers a practical review of metta, emphasizing that there is no single right way to practice and that body, phrases, and imagery can all serve kindness. He then moves through a detailed guided meditation, widening awareness through the whole body and using a ray-of-light image to support uprightness, connectedness, and ease. The talk closes by returning to lightness, gentleness, and patience as the right tone for practice.
short_summary: A detailed review of metta practice followed by a guided meditation that expands awareness through the body with light, gentle imagery.

chapters.title: Reviewing the practice
chapters.description: He reintroduces the three 'baskets' of metta practice and stresses that each approach is valid and healing.

chapters.title: Samadhi as spaciousness
chapters.description: He reframes concentration as a unification that is broad and balanced rather than tight or cramped.

chapters.title: Working with difficulty
chapters.description: He explains how to meet discomfort, pain, and blockage with flexibility, including imagination as a support for releasing contraction.

chapters.title: Whole-body metta
chapters.description: The practice expands from a focal point into the whole body, with metta expressed through touch, movement, and gentleness.

chapters.title: Boundless in steps
chapters.description: He places the practice in a larger arc: metta is boundless in aim, but approached gradually through suitable entry points.

chapters.title: Guided body meditation
chapters.description: He begins the sitting with contact, whole-body awareness, and a ray of light rising through the body, linking belly, chest, throat, and head.

chapters.title: Silent cultivation
chapters.description: Longer periods of silence are held within the same instructions to keep planting the intention of kindness and returning attention to the body.

chapters.title: Closing tone
chapters.description: He ends by emphasizing lightness, gentleness, and kindness in the effort itself, and invites the practice to remain soft and connected.

title: Second Instructions and Guided Metta Meditation
speaker: Rob Burbea
podcast_description: Rob Burbea offers a practical talk on metta as something responsive rather than rigid: sometimes steady, sometimes playful, sometimes set aside when difficulty becomes too strong. He distinguishes between ease, workable difficulty, and situations that call for a different kind of kindness altogether, then leads a long guided practice moving from posture and body awareness into self-metta and then metta for another person. The talk stays close to lived experience, with emphasis on warmth around difficulty, softening self-judgment, and trusting the varied textures of loving-kindness.
short_summary: A grounded teaching on responsive metta, including how to work with ease, difficulty, and a guided practice for self and other.

chapters.title: Responsive metta
chapters.description: Rob introduces the talk as a further look at metta and emphasizes responsiveness, lightness, and playfulness rather than heavy effort.

chapters.title: The sailing analogy
chapters.description: He uses sailing to show how practice changes with conditions, from smooth cruising to adjusting the sails as the wind shifts.

chapters.title: Working with difficulty
chapters.description: He names hindrances, restlessness, doubt, and sleepiness, then explains when to stay with metta and when to step back and meet suffering more directly.

chapters.title: Mindfulness with warmth
chapters.description: Rob points ahead to Catherine McGee's teaching and clarifies that mindfulness includes acceptance and kindness around hard experience, not just bare attention.

chapters.title: Phrases and resonance
chapters.description: He explains flexible use of phrases, the varying flavors of metta, and how words can echo in the body and be repeated only as long as they remain alive.

chapters.title: Allowing well-being
chapters.description: He explores the subtler possibility of drawing out or allowing bodily well-being, and how attention can choose not to get trapped by difficulty.

chapters.title: Self and easiest person
chapters.description: Rob distinguishes the narrative self from a simpler bodily sense of self, then prepares the group for a guided practice starting where metta feels easiest.

chapters.title: Guided metta practice
chapters.description: A long guided meditation settles posture, opens awareness through the body, and moves from self-metta to another person with phrases, imagery, and kinesthetic warmth.

chapters.title: Closing the practice
chapters.description: The guided practice continues through images and phrases until the meditation closes quietly at the end of the sit.

title: First Instructions and Guided Metta Meditation
speaker: Rob Burbea

title: Liberation and the Self - A Guided Inquiry
speaker: Rob Burbea
podcast_description: Rob Burbea offers a guided inquiry into liberation, freedom, and the self, beginning with a gentle explanation of how inquiry can uncover both hidden strengths and limiting beliefs. He then walks through a series of questions that invite listeners to examine what liberation means to them, how that idea affects their practice and life, and what self-view may be operating around it. The talk stays close to embodied experience throughout, emphasizing kindness, curiosity, and a willingness to trust whatever is actually present.
short_summary: A guided inquiry into liberation, freedom, and the self, rooted in body awareness, careful questioning, and kindness.

chapters.title: Opening the inquiry
chapters.description: Rob introduces the experiment of guided inquiry and explains that it can uncover both inner qualities and limiting assumptions.

chapters.title: Inquiry as an art
chapters.description: He describes inquiry as a practice that includes the body, emotions, and energy, using a social example to show how questioning can reveal contraction and movement.

chapters.title: How to work with it
chapters.description: He clarifies the ground rules: the process is private, nonjudgmental, and meant to stay alive in the present moment rather than repeat fixed conclusions.

chapters.title: First question: liberation
chapters.description: The first inquiry asks what liberation or freedom means, how the word resonates, and what it means if the concept feels absent, dull, or irritating.

chapters.title: Second question: its effects
chapters.description: He asks what effect one’s concept of liberation has on practice and life, including where it limits or constrains what freedom can mean.

chapters.title: Third question: self-view
chapters.description: The inquiry turns to how one sees oneself in relation to freedom, and whether those self-definitions feel true, helpful, or open to questioning.

chapters.title: Letting go of locking
chapters.description: The talk closes by inviting listeners to imagine loosening identification with these beliefs and to notice how that possibility feels in body and mind.

title: Metta Instructions and Guided Meditation 5
speaker: Rob Burbea
podcast_description: Rob Burbea offers practical metta instructions that move from the body, to difficulty, to an increasingly boundless field of kindness. He emphasizes that the practice is hard to do “wrong,” and keeps returning to flexibility: comfort in the body, use of phrases or visualization, and kindness toward whatever is present. In the second half, the talk opens into the Metta Sutta, a discussion of how metta changes perception, and a long guided meditation that widens from a bodily sense of ease to all beings.
short_summary: A practical metta talk that widens from bodily kindness into boundless goodwill for all beings.

chapters.title: Metta is forgiving
chapters.description: Rob begins by reassuring listeners that metta is difficult to get wrong, then frames the practice as gently leaning toward bodily well-being and ease.

chapters.title: Working with difficulty
chapters.description: He describes several ways to meet discomfort: stay with the phrases, open to bodily pain, allow spaciousness, or let kindness trickle into what feels blocked.

chapters.title: Care for the body
chapters.description: The instructions turn practical, including moving posture, avoiding getting pulled into pain, and using whole-body awareness to stay connected to what feels okay.

chapters.title: Energy and imagination
chapters.description: He explores subtle energy opening in practice and suggests using imagination, including images of flow, blockage, and tenderness through the hands and feet.

chapters.title: The Metta Sutta
chapters.description: Rob reads the Buddha's verses on loving-kindness and uses them to ground the retreat's direction toward boundless goodwill for all beings.

chapters.title: How metta changes perception
chapters.description: He outlines different ways to expand metta, then explains that as kindness deepens, perception changes and even ordinary problems like rain are colored by aversion or ease.

chapters.title: Guided practice begins
chapters.description: The guided meditation starts with bodily comfort, awareness, and a gentle invitation to offer kindness wherever it is easiest.

chapters.title: Opening to all beings
chapters.description: The meditation gradually expands into a sphere of kindness radiating in all directions, including human and non-human beings, with warmth and reverence.

chapters.title: Seeds without pressure
chapters.description: Rob keeps the practice light and persistent, reminding listeners that feeling or no feeling does not matter and that metta can be planted steadily with faith.

title: Metta Instructions and Guided Meditation 4
speaker: Rob Burbea
podcast_description: Rob Burbea offers metta as a practice of balance: not collapsing into suffering, but cultivating enough buoyancy, brightness, and steadiness to meet difficulty well. He moves from general guidance on how to approach a difficult person, into a guided meditation that begins with the easy places and gradually opens toward more complicated relationships. The talk closes with reminders about working skillfully with stories, returning to self-kindness, and carrying metta into everyday movement.
short_summary: A guided metta talk on balancing buoyancy with compassion, especially when working with difficult people.

chapters.title: Balancing metta and compassion
chapters.description: Burbea introduces metta as a practice of balance, where kindness can shade into compassion without losing buoyancy.

chapters.title: Approaching difficult people
chapters.description: He explains the flexible categories of easy, neutral, and difficult people, and why it is often wiser to begin where the heart can stay open.

chapters.title: What to reflect on
chapters.description: The talk offers reflections on shared vulnerability, ignorance, and the ways hurting others also wounds oneself.

chapters.title: Working with hurt and anger
chapters.description: He suggests alternating between the other person and oneself when difficult feelings arise, and seeing the relationship more impersonally and humanly.

chapters.title: Settling into meditation
chapters.description: The guided practice begins with posture, bodily energy, softening, and then directing metta first where it feels easiest.

chapters.title: Opening to a difficult person
chapters.description: The practice shifts toward someone difficult, starting with noticing the heart's response and reflecting on commonality, vulnerability, and confusion.

chapters.title: Spacious, patient effort
chapters.description: Burbea encourages lightness rather than strain, and broadens the practice to include memory of past kindness or imagining the person receiving love.

chapters.title: Story and self-definition
chapters.description: He asks listeners to notice the stories binding self and other, and to see whether those narratives are helpful or can be gently released.

chapters.title: Returning and carrying on
chapters.description: The meditation returns to self-mettta, then closes with encouragement to continue the practice in daily life and to move slowly when walking.

title: Metta Instructions and Guided Meditation 3
speaker: Rob Burbea
podcast_description: Rob Burbea first speaks about why receiving kindness can feel harder than giving it, and suggests ways to notice support from nature, community, and one’s own metta practice. He then introduces the day’s new categories—the neutral person and the difficult person—offering reflections on common humanity, mortality, and the difference between metta and compassion before beginning a guided meditation. The meditation moves gently through posture, self-metta, the neutral person, and the closing return to the body.
short_summary: A metta talk and guided meditation focused on receiving, the neutral person, and introducing the difficult person.

chapters.title: Receiving Kindness
chapters.description: Rob explores why receiving love can feel harder than giving it, and points to support already present in nature, retreat life, and self-metta.

chapters.title: Metta And Energy
chapters.description: He introduces the practice direction for the day, including how sexual energy can be worked with in the body and softened toward metta.

chapters.title: Neutral Person Reflections
chapters.description: Rob explains the neutral person, using common humanity, mortality, and vulnerability to make the practice more vivid and less flat.

chapters.title: Settling For Practice
chapters.description: The guided meditation begins with posture, openness, and a broad body awareness before any specific phrase of metta.

chapters.title: Self Metta And Easiest Person
chapters.description: He invites loving-kindness first toward whatever feels easiest, allowing the practice to become warm, tender, and playful.

chapters.title: Neutral Person Practice
chapters.description: The meditation turns to the neutral person, emphasizing shared wishes for well-being and the common fragility of human life.

chapters.title: Closing And Return
chapters.description: After periods of silent metta and repetition, the practice ends with a sense of preciousness and a return to simple bodily awareness.

title: Metta Instructions and Guided Meditation 2
speaker: Rob Burbea
podcast_description: Rob Burbea offers a detailed set of metta instructions, emphasizing a light touch: gentle effort, responsiveness, and flexibility rather than pressure. He walks through how to work with hindrances, how the phrases can be used in different ways, and how metta may show up as calm, warmth, brightness, compassion, or even no clear feeling at all. The guided practice then moves from body awareness to self or benefactor, and finally to a friend, with imaginative ways of supporting loving-kindness throughout.
short_summary: A grounded set of metta instructions and a guided practice centered on lightness, responsiveness, and widening the circle from self to friend.

chapters.title: Setting the tone
chapters.description: Burbea introduces the day’s focus and frames metta as light, spacious, playful, and responsive rather than effortful.

chapters.title: Working with hindrances
chapters.description: He names common obstacles like sadness, sleepiness, doubt, restlessness, and anger, and suggests flexible ways to meet them without abandoning metta.

chapters.title: Flexible practice forms
chapters.description: The talk shifts to the practical range inside metta: choosing categories, using what is easiest, and varying phrases from repeated sentences to a single word or silence.

chapters.title: What metta can feel like
chapters.description: He describes metta as a broad field of qualities—calming, healing, warm, bright, gentle, bubbly, or compassion-colored—and normalizes the absence of a strong feeling.

chapters.title: Body and resonance
chapters.description: Burbea emphasizes sensitivity to the whole body, listening for how different phrases resonate, and noticing subtle bodily warmth or openness without forcing it.

chapters.title: Simple self and basics
chapters.description: He explains the move from narrative self toward a simpler body-being sense, then introduces the friend category and begins the guided practice.

chapters.title: Using imagination
chapters.description: During the practice he encourages visual and kinesthetic imagination—warmth, softness, light, and tenderness—as a way to support the heart’s response.

chapters.title: Metta for a friend
chapters.description: The guided meditation turns to a friend, evoking their goodness and wish for well-being, and extending loving-kindness through images, touch, and radiating warmth.

chapters.title: Closing the practice
chapters.description: The meditation winds down by letting the sense of care settle, then returning to the body and ending quietly.

title: Metta Instructions and Guided Meditation 1
speaker: Rob Burbea
podcast_description: Rob Burbea introduces a metta practice centered on self-kindness and the benefactor, with a strong emphasis on flexibility rather than formula. He explains how to work with phrases, imagery, and body-sense, and why the practice is more about planting intentions than manufacturing a feeling. The talk then moves into a guided meditation, first for oneself and then for a benefactor, with guidance on walking practice at the end.
short_summary: A practical introduction to metta that moves from principles and phrasing into a guided practice for self and benefactor.

chapters.title: Metta and Boundlessness
chapters.description: Burbea reviews metta as unconditional and boundless, and explains the day’s structure of beginning with self and benefactor.

chapters.title: Finding the Benefactor
chapters.description: He defines the benefactor as simply the easiest person to wish well, rather than necessarily a heroic figure.

chapters.title: Playful Practice
chapters.description: The talk opens up the range of methods, encouraging creativity and introducing phrases, visualization, and body-sense.

chapters.title: Working with Phrases
chapters.description: He offers sample phrases, warns against turning them into a corrective agenda, and stresses finding words that genuinely fit.

chapters.title: Feeling and Intention
chapters.description: Burbea explains that metta feelings are impermanent and that the deeper work is to plant seeds of intention with patience and faith.

chapters.title: Self Metta Guided
chapters.description: The guided practice begins with settling the body, centering awareness, and offering phrases of well-wishing to oneself.

chapters.title: Self Kindness Continues
chapters.description: The meditation continues with repeated phrases, tenderness toward the body, and reassurance that the feeling itself may come and go.

chapters.title: Turning to the Benefactor
chapters.description: He shifts from self to benefactor, inviting an easy image of someone who naturally evokes gratitude and kindness.

chapters.title: Imagery and Walking
chapters.description: The talk closes with refinements on visual and kinesthetic approaches, then offers ways to continue metta in walking meditation and daily body practice.

title: Guided Meditation: The Vastness of Awareness
speaker: Rob Burbea
description: (Gaia House) Please note that these talks are from a 4 week retreat for experienced meditators. The talks and meditations can be listened to in any order or individually, but as they progressively unfold different levels of understanding of Emptiness, they will probably be more fully understood and the practices more easily developed if taken in series.
podcast_description: This guided meditation begins by settling the body and gathering attention, then gradually opens awareness to sound, silence, and the sense of spaciousness itself. Rob Burbea uses repeated imagery—open sky, ocean, waves, reflections, and fireflies—to invite a felt sense that appearances arise and fade within an unshakable field of awareness. The closing turns to how to practice this in walking, standing, and with eyes open or closed.
short_summary: A guided meditation that moves from bodily settling into vast, receptive awareness and a quiet re-seeing of experience as fleeting appearances.

chapters.title: Settling In
chapters.description: The meditation opens with posture, centering, and calming body and mind.

chapters.title: Body and Stillness
chapters.description: Attention is gathered in the body, including the life and stillness already present there.

chapters.title: Opening to Sound
chapters.description: Awareness widens from bodily stillness to listening, sound, and the space around sound.

chapters.title: Silence as Ground
chapters.description: The practice turns toward silence between sounds and the sense of sounds arising from and returning to silence.

chapters.title: Sky of Awareness
chapters.description: Awareness is imagined as an open sky in which sensations, thoughts, and images appear and disappear.

chapters.title: Letting Everything Belong
chapters.description: The instruction softens into non-struggle, non-control, and allowing every appearance to belong to awareness.

chapters.title: Waves and Reflections
chapters.description: More images—waves, a lake, floating, dissolving—emphasize experience as insubstantial and free within awareness.

chapters.title: Repeated Refrain
chapters.description: The meditation keeps returning to letting all experience arise and fade, seen again and again as impressions in awareness.

chapters.title: Pervading Practice
chapters.description: The talk concludes by pointing to the stillness, purity, and peace of awareness, then suggests bringing the same view into walking, standing, and open- or closed-eye practice.

title: Guided Meditation: Three Characteristics
speaker: Rob Burbea
description: (Gaia House) Please note that these talks are from a 4 week retreat for experienced meditators. The talks and meditations can be listened to in any order or individually, but as they progressively unfold different levels of understanding of Emptiness, they will probably be more fully understood and the practices more easily developed if taken in series.
podcast_description: Rob Burbea leads a guided meditation on the three characteristics, beginning with settling the body and moving through impermanence, reactivity, and not-self. The practice shifts from narrow attention on sensations to a wider field that includes the body, sounds, and the visual field, then into a gentler way of letting experience be. In the closing review, he emphasizes balancing samadhi and insight and warns against turning practice into a search for special experiences.
short_summary: A guided exploration of impermanence, dukkha, and not-self, framed as a flexible way of relating to experience rather than chasing meditation states.

chapters.title: Settling In
chapters.description: He begins by collecting attention in the body and calming the mind enough to start the contemplations.

chapters.title: Impermanence in the Body
chapters.description: Attention is directed to the hands and face, noticing changing sensations and the fact of fluctuation itself.

chapters.title: Wider Field of Change
chapters.description: The contemplation expands to other body areas and then to the whole body, with a strong emphasis on flickering, pulsing impermanence.

chapters.title: Reactivity and Dukkha
chapters.description: He introduces pleasant, unpleasant, and neutral feeling tones, and asks listeners to notice aversion and craving as bodily contraction.

chapters.title: Letting Be
chapters.description: The emphasis shifts from careful noticing to allowing experience to be as it is, rather than forcing release.

chapters.title: Not-Self Practice
chapters.description: He guides a third contemplation in which sensations, sounds, thoughts, and even awareness are seen as just happening, not me or mine.

chapters.title: Seeing Without Clinging
chapters.description: The hand-and-eyes exercise is repeated to explore the subtle felt difference between identification and a lighter, more spacious way of seeing.

chapters.title: Balancing the Practice
chapters.description: He reviews the three characteristics work, encourages a flexible balance with samadhi, and cautions against hunting for experiences.

chapters.title: Two Ways of Not-Self
chapters.description: In response to a question, he contrasts self-inquiry with a more direct seeing that everything is simply happening, including awareness itself.

title: The Space of Awareness - Welcoming (4th Guided Meditation)
speaker: Rob Burbea
podcast_description: In this guided meditation, Rob Burbea leads a steady practice of welcoming whatever is most present, beginning with posture, bodily balance, and a gentle aspiration toward kindness. The attention gradually widens from a single sensation or mood to the whole body, then to sounds, the full field of experience, and even the subtle sense of resistance itself. As the practice deepens, he emphasizes relaxing the tension of aversion and clinging, and resting in a spacious openness that can include blur, fading, and change.
short_summary: A guided meditation on welcoming every aspect of experience into spacious, kind awareness.

chapters.title: Settling the posture
chapters.description: The meditation begins with finding a balanced posture that holds wakefulness and softness together.

chapters.title: Setting kindness
chapters.description: Rob invites a clear aspiration and asks listeners to notice what is most prominent right now.

chapters.title: Welcoming what arises
chapters.description: Whatever appears—body sensation, mood, emotion, discomfort, or something pleasant—is met with complete welcoming.

chapters.title: Opening the whole body
chapters.description: The practice expands to include the whole body and whole mind as a field of total acceptance.

chapters.title: Including sounds and space
chapters.description: Awareness widens further to near and distant sounds and to the sense of vast space for everything to be allowed.

chapters.title: Resting in allowance
chapters.description: The emphasis shifts to sitting back into total welcoming, regardless of whether experience is pleasant, unpleasant, or neutral.

chapters.title: Returning to welcoming
chapters.description: After a brief acknowledgment of related practices, the instruction returns to repeatedly softening into openness and letting phenomena arise, stay, and disappear.

chapters.title: Working with resistance
chapters.description: Rob points to subtle aversion, clinging, and tension in the body or in the felt space of experience, and shows how to relax them.

chapters.title: Welcoming subtlety
chapters.description: The meditation becomes increasingly refined, including the image of the self, the welcoming itself, and the fading or blurring of boundaries, ending in repeated allowing.

title: The Space of Awareness - Only Impressions in Awareness (3rd Guided Meditation)
speaker: Rob Burbea
podcast_description: This guided meditation moves from settling the body and remembering kindness into sensing stillness, silence, and spacious awareness. Rob Burbea then uses a series of imaginal analogies—sky, fireflies, waves, reflections, bubbles—to point to experience as appearing and fading within awareness, without needing to be grasped or controlled. The final section gently extends the practice into daily postures and situations, inviting exploration beyond the sitting meditation.
short_summary: A guided meditation on resting in the open space of awareness and seeing all experience as fleeting impressions within it.

chapters.title: Opening the retreat field
chapters.description: He begins by noting that the afternoon meditations may seem different, while pointing to them as different ways into the same territory.

chapters.title: Settling with kindness
chapters.description: The practice starts with posture, kindness, aspiration, and letting the body gather into calm.

chapters.title: Sensing stillness
chapters.description: Attention is guided toward bodily stillness and the stillness that can be felt in the whole field of experience.

chapters.title: Listening into silence
chapters.description: Awareness opens to sound, then to the silence around and between sounds, as if silence were the ground of hearing.

chapters.title: Sky of awareness
chapters.description: He introduces the image of awareness as open sky, with sensations and thoughts like brief lights, clouds, or fireworks passing through it.

chapters.title: Nothing to hold
chapters.description: The teaching emphasizes that whatever appears can be received naturally, without rejection, clinging, or interference, while awareness remains undisturbed.

chapters.title: Impressions in awareness
chapters.description: The imagery shifts to waves in the ocean and reflections on a lake, pointing to all experience as an impression that arises and fades within awareness.

chapters.title: Self as appearance
chapters.description: Even the sense of self and the image of oneself meditating are treated as further appearances, not something solid to identify with.

chapters.title: Qualities and aftercare
chapters.description: He closes by naming the vastness, peace, and receptivity of awareness, then suggests taking the practice into walking, standing, and ordinary settings.

title: The Space of Awareness - Silence (2nd Guided Meditation)
speaker: Rob Burbea
podcast_description: In this guided meditation, Rob Burbea leads a gradual settling into the space of awareness through posture, kindness, and listening. The practice widens from bare sound to the whole field of hearing, then to body sensations, thoughts, and images, emphasizing openness, presence, and non-clinging. He repeatedly turns toward silence as something that can be sensed within and around sound, until everything is invited to rest in that spaciousness.
short_summary: A guided meditation on opening awareness through sound, body, thought, and the silence at the heart of experience.

chapters.title: Settling the posture
chapters.description: The instruction begins by orienting toward expansive awareness, with careful attention to posture, balance, and a kind inner direction.

chapters.title: Kindness and grounding
chapters.description: He emphasizes kindness as the compass for practice and encourages a grounded sense of the seated body.

chapters.title: Listening to sound
chapters.description: The practice shifts to open, bare listening, receiving sound without naming or figuring it out.

chapters.title: Global sound field
chapters.description: Awareness widens to the totality of sound, with a balance of receptive spaciousness and vivid presence.

chapters.title: Sounds arising and passing
chapters.description: He points to sounds coming and going on their own, with no need to reject or cling to anything heard.

chapters.title: Body in awareness
chapters.description: Body sensations are included in the same open field, appearing and disappearing within vast receptive awareness.

chapters.title: Everything is included
chapters.description: The practice expands to thoughts and images as well, with an emphasis on equal treatment, heartfulness, and listening with the whole being.

chapters.title: Silence within sound
chapters.description: He invites a sense of silence as a quality around, between, and within sounds, rather than as the absence of experience.

chapters.title: Belonging to silence
