chapters.description: The closing instructions deepen into stillness embracing the body and all phenomena, then end with a reminder to trust what feels opening and helpful.

title: Breath Meditation (2nd Instructions/Guided Meditation)
speaker: Rob Burbea
podcast_description: Rob Burbea leads a long guided breath meditation that begins with posture, kindness, and widening the field of awareness through the whole body. He then invites listeners to explore the breath from several centers—below the navel, the heart, throat, mid-head, crown, base of spine, upper back, and elsewhere—treating each as an experiment in what feels most open, comfortable, or enjoyable. The final section shifts into practical advice for working with pain and discomfort, emphasizing relaxation, spaciousness, imagination, and the freedom to change posture when needed.
short_summary: A guided exploration of breath and bodily awareness, followed by practical instruction for meeting discomfort with spaciousness and kindness.

chapters.title: Opening and setup
chapters.description: A brief opening joke and introduction to a second guided meditation on the breath.

chapters.title: Posture and kindness
chapters.description: The practice begins with posture, body presence, and establishing a kind, receptive attitude.

chapters.title: Whole-body awareness
chapters.description: Awareness is broadened to fill the whole body, using images of a balloon or sail filled by wind.

chapters.title: Lower belly and right side
chapters.description: The breath is explored at the lower abdomen, first on the left and then on the right, with attention to what feels best.

chapters.title: Midline and heart
chapters.description: The center moves to the midline and then up to the heart and throat, with encouragement to experiment with breath length and subtlety.

chapters.title: Head and crown centers
chapters.description: The practice shifts to the middle of the head and then the crown, inviting luminous, expansive breath awareness.

chapters.title: Base and upper back
chapters.description: The center drops to the base of the spine and then rises to the upper back, continuing the theme of flexible exploration.

chapters.title: Review and possibilities
chapters.description: Rob reflects on the range of possible approaches to breath and breath-energy practice and how different forms help different people.

chapters.title: Working with discomfort
chapters.description: He gives practical advice for pain and constriction: relax the rest of the body, keep awareness spacious, use imagination, and change posture when needed.

title: The Space of Awareness - Impermanence (1st Guided Meditation)
speaker: Rob Burbea
podcast_description: In this guided meditation, Rob Burbea invites a spacious, open awareness and turns it toward impermanence: first in a single area of body sensation, then across the whole body, then into sounds, thoughts, and the full field of experience. The practice stays close to bare change—arising, passing, flickering—while encouraging an alert but relaxed attention that can support letting go. Near the end, he reflects on the wider teaching context and notes that this is one of two practices to be explored in different ways across the retreat.
short_summary: A guided exploration of impermanence in spacious awareness, moving from body sensations to sounds, thoughts, and the whole field of experience.

chapters.title: Opening the practice
chapters.description: Burbea introduces the spacious-awareness practice, explains its relation to the breath work, and frames impermanence as the lens for this sitting.

chapters.title: Settling the body
chapters.description: The meditation begins with posture, intention, and a single changing area of body sensation, with attention resting on bare sensation and its fluctuation.

chapters.title: Widening to the body
chapters.description: Attention expands from one area to the whole body, noticing the global pattern of sensations as coming and going within a larger field.

chapters.title: Including sound
chapters.description: The field opens further to sounds, which are also held as bare, changing events arising and vanishing in space.

chapters.title: Only the change
chapters.description: He keeps redirecting practice away from content and back to the texture of change, even when thoughts or inner material arise.

chapters.title: Working with difficulty
chapters.description: Areas that feel contracted or problematic are opened into a wider space, so that their shifting patterns can be seen as part of impermanence.

chapters.title: Everything in the field
chapters.description: The instruction broadens to include all experience—outer, inner, thoughts, images—while staying with the fact of change itself.

chapters.title: Space and flicker
chapters.description: Burbea emphasizes the steadiness of awareness itself and repeatedly points back to the flickering, sparkling quality of all phenomena within it.

chapters.title: Letting go, closing
chapters.description: Seeing impermanence is linked with letting be and letting go, and the talk closes by situating this as one of two ongoing retreat practices.

title: Breath Meditation (1st Instructions and Guided Meditation)
speaker: Rob Burbea
podcast_description: Rob Burbea offers a guided breath meditation that begins with posture and bodily ease, then gradually widens into a whole-body awareness of breath, energy, and comfort. He repeatedly invites the listener to experiment gently with long or short breathing, to include subtle pleasure or ease when it is present, and to keep returning with patience when the mind wanders. Later in the talk he pauses the meditation to explain why this approach matters: it can build a quiet inner resource, support calm and brightness, and make insight more available.
short_summary: A gentle, experimental breath meditation that uses posture, relaxation, and whole-body awareness to cultivate ease, energy, and steady attention.

chapters.title: Settling the posture
chapters.description: The meditation begins with posture, uprightness, softness, and easing tension in the face, throat, shoulders, chest, and belly.

chapters.title: Kindness and body space
chapters.description: Rob aligns the practice with kindness and opens awareness to the space of the body before introducing the breath.

chapters.title: Long breath, whole body
chapters.description: The breath is made long and smooth, with attention to whole-body expansion, contraction, energizing, and relaxing.

chapters.title: Choosing what helps
chapters.description: He encourages a light, receptive attention and invites listeners to notice whatever is most helpful, including subtle pleasure or comfort.

chapters.title: Why practice this way
chapters.description: The talk shifts from instruction into explanation, showing how breath practice can support energy, well-being, calm, and insight.

chapters.title: Six practical reminders
chapters.description: Rob reviews the main points: kindness, experimentation, not preconstruing the breath, including comfort, expanding awareness, and staying present.

chapters.title: Patience and adjustment
chapters.description: He closes by emphasizing enjoyment without pressure, patience with distraction, and adjusting the breath to what is actually appropriate in the moment.

title: Guided Meditation - Cittamatra
speaker: Rob Burbea
description: (Gaia House) Please note that these talks are from a 4 week retreat for experienced meditators. The talks and meditations can be listened to in any order or individually, but as they progressively unfold different levels of understanding of Emptiness, they will probably be more fully understood and the practices more easily developed if taken in series
podcast_description: This guided meditation develops a quiet, spacious way of attending in which body, sound, silence, and thought are all received without interference. Rob Burbea repeatedly returns to the imagery of awareness as sky-like and imperturbable, then later reframes all experience—including the sense of self—as an impression in awareness. Near the end, he connects the practice to three characteristics work and offers a few practical ways to try it with eyes open or closed.
short_summary: A guided meditation on awareness as open sky, where phenomena, silence, and self-sense are all seen as passing impressions.

chapters.title: Settling the body
chapters.description: The meditation begins by settling awareness into the body and nurturing calm through breath, mettā, or another familiar support.

chapters.title: Body and stillness
chapters.description: Attention deepens into bodily sensation, with an emphasis on filling the body with awareness and sensing stillness permeating it.

chapters.title: Sound and silence
chapters.description: Awareness opens to sound, then to the silence around and within sound, as a way of sensing the relationship between appearance and stillness.

chapters.title: Awareness as sky
chapters.description: Burbea introduces the image of mind like a clear sky, spacious enough to receive sensations, sounds, and thoughts without strain.

chapters.title: Letting things be
chapters.description: The practice turns toward non-struggle, non-rejection, and non-control, while emphasizing that whatever arises is embraced and undisturbed.

chapters.title: Impressions in awareness
chapters.description: Experience is reframed as appearances, reflections, or impressions in awareness, including body sensations, thoughts, images, and the sense of self.

chapters.title: Waves on the ocean
chapters.description: He extends the imagery with waves and ocean, showing experience as one substance taking different forms and then dissolving back again.

chapters.title: Three characteristics and practice
chapters.description: The talk closes by linking the imagery to three characteristics practice and suggesting ways to explore it with eyes open, eyes closed, walking, or standing.

title: Guided Meditation on the Three Characteristics
speaker: Rob Burbea
description: (Gaia House) Please note that these talks are from a 4 week retreat for experienced meditators. The talks and meditations can be listened to in any order or individually, but as they progressively unfold different levels of understanding of Emptiness, they will probably be more fully understood and the practices more easily developed if taken in series
podcast_description: This guided meditation introduces three ways of working with experience: attending closely to impermanence, softening aversion or grasping, and seeing sensations, sounds, and thoughts as not-self. The practice moves through the hands, face, whole body, sound, sight, and thought, with brief pauses to let the sense of change become clearer. In the closing section, Rob also speaks about the unfamiliarity that can arise as letting go deepens, and how to meet fear by inclining toward what is peaceful, spacious, or warm.
short_summary: A guided exploration of impermanence, allowing, and not-self across body, sound, vision, and thought.

chapters.title: Three approaches
chapters.description: Rob introduces the three linked modes of practice and sets the tone for a relaxed, balanced meditation posture.

chapters.title: Settling the mind
chapters.description: The meditation begins with calming and gathering awareness through breath, metta, or open awareness.

chapters.title: Impermanence in the hands
chapters.description: Attention moves into the hands, where the main emphasis is on noticing flicker, pulse, and change.

chapters.title: Face and whole body
chapters.description: The practice broadens to the face and then the whole body, still resting on the changing nature of sensation.

chapters.title: Sound and relation
chapters.description: Awareness opens to sound and then to the felt relationship with pleasant and unpleasant experience, including aversion and grasping.

chapters.title: Allowing experience
chapters.description: Rob reframes the practice as letting experience be, emphasizing allowing and non-interference.

chapters.title: Not-self in awareness
chapters.description: The third approach is introduced: seeing sensations and sounds as just happening, with less ownership and identification.

chapters.title: Walking through the day
chapters.description: The same three approaches are applied to movement and daily life, followed by a brief review of the methods.

chapters.title: Fear and familiarization
chapters.description: Rob speaks about the fear that can arise as consciousness opens, and suggests meeting it by trusting small doses of spaciousness, peace, or joy.

title: Second Morning Instructions and Guided Meditation
speaker: Rob Burbea
description: (Gaia House) As we learn to develop concentration in meditation, samatha (calm, tranquility) is also developed, and together these qualities become a powerful means for deep insight and a source of profound well-being. This progressive series of talks, guided meditations and instructions explores in some detail the art of concentration, primarily through different ways of working with the breath and the body to open to deeper and deeper levels of calmness, presence and joy.
podcast_description: Rob Burbea offers a second-morning review of concentration practice, emphasizing whole-body awareness, sensitivity, steadiness, and working skillfully with discomfort. He then lays out a set of practical options for pain, restlessness, dullness, doubt, and craving, framing each as something to meet with experimentation rather than force. The talk closes with a long guided meditation that moves the breath through different points in the body, from the lower belly to the crown and feet, always returning to a whole-body field of awareness.
short_summary: A practical review of samatha instructions followed by a long guided meditation on whole-body breath sensitivity.

chapters.title: Reviewing the basics
chapters.description: Burbea recaps the central themes from the previous evening, especially whole-body awareness, experimenting with the breath, and balancing calm with energy.

chapters.title: Sensitivity and steadiness
chapters.description: He develops the paired qualities of sensitivity and steadiness, describing how attention can become more alive and less easily thrown off by ups and downs.

chapters.title: Working with discomfort
chapters.description: The talk turns to practical ways of meeting pain and constriction, including relaxing the rest of the body, enlarging awareness, and staying with comfort.

chapters.title: Breath as a resource
chapters.description: He explores using breath and imagination to soothe blocked areas, move energy through the body, and notice how often the most constricted place can open into the most pleasant.

chapters.title: Hindrances and discernment
chapters.description: Burbea addresses fear, tiredness, restlessness, doubt, greed, and aversion, and briefly invokes the Buddha’s pragmatic question about what is helpful and what leads to freedom.

chapters.title: Settling the posture
chapters.description: The guided meditation begins by settling into posture and reconnecting practice with goodwill, kindness, and a whole-body sense of sensitivity.

chapters.title: Breath through the body
chapters.description: He leads attention through breath at the lower belly, hara, solar plexus, heart center, throat, head, and crown, inviting the breath to be felt in whatever way is most supportive.

chapters.title: Down the body and feet
chapters.description: The meditation expands into breath moving down through the body, out through the feet, and into the back and spine, with repeated emphasis on what feels best right now.

chapters.title: Centering and spaciousness
chapters.description: The practice closes by centering awareness in the middle of the body, imagining the breath as arising from within, and then resting wherever feels most balanced and alive.

title: First Morning Instructions and Guided Meditation
speaker: Rob Burbea
description: (Gaia House) As we learn to develop concentration in meditation, samatha (calm, tranquility) is also developed, and together these qualities become a powerful means for deep insight and a source of profound well-being. This progressive series of talks, guided meditations and instructions explores in some detail the art of concentration, primarily through different ways of working with the breath and the body to open to deeper and deeper levels of calmness, presence and joy.
podcast_description: Rob Burbea gives a guided meditation that begins with posture, relaxing the face, throat, shoulders, and belly, then opens into wide whole-body awareness. He gradually works with the breath as a gentle way to energize, soothe, and calm the body, and then shifts into finding a place of comfort and letting it be nourished without pressure. Near the end, he recaps the method as an exploratory practice of patience, responsiveness, and contentment, with comfort allowed to grow slowly and spread through the body.
short_summary: A guided concentration practice centered on whole-body awareness, responsive breathing, and gently nourishing comfort.

chapters.title: Settling the posture
chapters.description: He begins by orienting the session and guiding a careful settling into an upright, open, relaxed posture.

chapters.title: Whole-body awareness
chapters.description: Awareness expands to the body as a whole, with attention to contact sensations and a broad, permeating presence.

chapters.title: Working with the breath
chapters.description: The breath is lengthened and explored in a large field of awareness, noticing its effects through the torso, throat, spine, and legs.

chapters.title: Breath as nourishment
chapters.description: He turns to using the breath to open, energize, soothe, and calm the body according to what is needed in the moment.

chapters.title: Play without forcing
chapters.description: Rob recaps the practice as playful experiment rather than control, encouraging questions like whether the breath wants to be longer, shorter, stronger, or more refined.

chapters.title: Comfort that can spread
chapters.description: He closes by emphasizing contentment with any small comfort that appears, allowing it to be supported gently and, when steady, to spread through the body.

title: Guided Meditation - Directing Love Towards dharmas
speaker: Rob Burbea
description: (Gaia House) This series of talks and guided meditations explores the development of the practices of both Lovingkindness and Compassion, with particular emphasis on the radical possibilities of Awakening that they bring. Through these practices we come to develop deep and beautiful qualities of heart as a real resource both for ourselves and the world, and also open ourselves to the profound and liberating understandings that can emerge from this path of love.
podcast_description: This guided meditation begins by settling the body and opening attention to whatever is most prominent in experience. It then repeatedly invites that experience to be bathed, held, and totally welcomed in kindness and compassion, expanding the practice to include resistance, numbness, peace, happiness, and even the intention to be kind. The closing turns toward a quiet inquiry: whether anything exists independent of our relationship to it.
short_summary: A guided meditation on welcoming whatever arises in awareness with kindness, then reflecting on relationship as central to experience.

chapters.title: Settling the body
chapters.description: The meditation begins with posture, openness, and softening into the body and the field of bodily sensation.

chapters.title: Meeting what stands out
chapters.description: Attention turns to whatever is prominent in experience, whether sensation, emotion, thought, or mind state.

chapters.title: Bathing experience in kindness
chapters.description: Rob introduces the core practice of directing lovingkindness and compassion toward whatever is present, including complete welcoming and radical acceptance.

chapters.title: Letting precision soften
chapters.description: The emphasis shifts from careful noticing to spacious kindness, repeating the invitation to hold each experience without trying to exclude anything.

chapters.title: Nothing is exempt
chapters.description: After a brief stretch of referenced names, the guidance expands to include resistance, numbness, boredom, fear, restlessness, peace, and happiness alike.

chapters.title: Including self and intention
chapters.description: The practice widens further to include the image of oneself, then even the kindness itself and the intention toward kindness.

chapters.title: Relationship and inquiry
chapters.description: The meditation closes with a repeated question about whether anything exists independent of our relationship to it.

title: Guided Meditation on Gratitude and Compassion
speaker: Rob Burbea
description: (Gaia House) This series of talks and guided meditations explores the development of the practices of both Lovingkindness and Compassion, with particular emphasis on the radical possibilities of Awakening that they bring. Through these practices we come to develop deep and beautiful qualities of heart as a real resource both for ourselves and the world, and also open ourselves to the profound and liberating understandings that can emerge from this path of love.
podcast_description: This guided meditation moves from settling the body into a widening contemplation of gratitude and compassion. It begins with a benefactor, then expands through teachers, parents, friends, difficult people, strangers, and finally the whole web of human and more-than-human life. Rob Burbea closes by offering simple ways to practice compassion for all beings, including an option to let the body radiate compassion in space.
short_summary: A guided meditation that expands gratitude into compassion for benefactors, loved ones, difficult people, and all beings.

chapters.title: Settling the Body
chapters.description: The meditation begins by finding ease, uprightness, and an open sensitivity to bodily experience.

chapters.title: Benefactor and Compassion
chapters.description: A benefactor is brought to mind, first in gratitude for their kindness and then in compassion for their suffering.

chapters.title: Many Who Have Helped
chapters.description: The practice widens to include other kind people, spiritual teachers, and school teachers, with appreciation and compassion for each.

chapters.title: Parents and Early Care
chapters.description: The heart turns toward parents and the care they provided, alongside sensitivity to the complexity and pain in those relationships.

chapters.title: Friends and Intimacy
chapters.description: Friends and partners are recollected, including the gifts of companionship and intimacy, and their own struggles are held in compassion.

chapters.title: Unknown Benefactors
chapters.description: The meditation expands to unknown inventors, scientists, artists, and workers whose efforts shape daily life.

chapters.title: Trade and Difficult People
chapters.description: The web of cooperation and exchange is reflected on, then the practice turns toward difficult people and the growth they can evoke.

chapters.title: Animals and Ecosystem
chapters.description: Compassion opens to animals, plants, and the fragile interconnectedness of the ecosystem, ending with a spacious radiating of compassion.

chapters.title: Practice Options and Close
chapters.description: Burbea offers practical ways to include all beings in compassion and suggests experimenting with categories, directions, or bodily radiance.

title: Third Samatha Instructions and Guided Meditation
speaker: Rob Burbea
podcast_description: Rob Burbea offers a guided samatha practice that moves the center of awareness through different places in the body, asking what kind of breath feels most easeful and nourishing at each point. He frames the practice as an experiment in sensitivity rather than a fixed method, and pauses afterward to explain how body-centered attention can support pleasure, steadiness, and whole-body awareness. In the Q&A, he also addresses difficult emotions, the spider-web image for centered awareness, and practical issues like a blocked nose.
short_summary: A guided samatha meditation on shifting the center of awareness through the body, followed by teaching and questions about using the practice skillfully.

chapters.title: Settling the Posture
chapters.description: The meditation opens with posture, ease, and scanning the body for obvious tension.

chapters.title: Lower Belly Center
chapters.description: Awareness is stationed in the lower abdomen, exploring the most comfortable breath and how the rest of the body responds.

chapters.title: Right Side and Belly
chapters.description: The center shifts to the right side of the lower belly, then deeper into the lower abdomen, with very light attention to bodily effects.

chapters.title: Solar Plexus and Heart
chapters.description: The practice moves upward through the solar plexus and heart center, including a suggestion to ease constriction and invite okayness.

chapters.title: Throat to Crown
chapters.description: Attention travels through the throat, the middle of the head, the crown, and the top of the spine, each time asking how the breath wants to be there.

chapters.title: Spine and Return
chapters.description: The base of the spine is explored, then the practice returns to one of the central body locations for a settled whole-body awareness.

chapters.title: After the Sit
chapters.description: Rob reflects on moving awareness around the body, the spider-web analogy, and how to work with difficult emotion without making the practice rigid.

chapters.title: Questions and Context
chapters.description: He clarifies traditional breath practices, discusses a simple mudra for blocked breathing, and explains how samatha can soothe emotion while staying part of a wider practice.

chapters.title: Closing Instructions
chapters.description: The talk ends with encouragement about group process, a short announcement, and a transition into walking meditation with appreciation.

title: Second Samatha Instructions and Guided Meditation
speaker: Rob Burbea
podcast_description: Rob Burbea offers practical samatha instructions, moving from the long breath to finding the breath that is most comfortable, enjoyable, and supportive of collectedness. He emphasizes keeping the whole body in awareness, balancing energizing and calming, and working skillfully with hindrances, self-judgment, and the tendency to fixate on what is difficult. The talk then turns to discomfort in sitting, with several ways to work with pain before ending in a guided settling into posture and breath, followed later by a stretch of repeated literary and teacher references, especially Rainer Maria Rilke.
short_summary: A clear set of samatha instructions on breath, whole-body awareness, and working skillfully with hindrances and sitting pain.

chapters.title: Gathering the instructions
chapters.description: He opens by collecting the retreat's samatha points into a single set of instructions and introduces the long breath as the starting place.

chapters.title: Balancing energy and calm
chapters.description: The talk explains how to adjust breath, attention, and bodily awareness so practice stays balanced between energizing and calming.

chapters.title: Working with hindrances
chapters.description: He names the hindrances, especially sloth and torpor or restlessness, and encourages less judgment and less story around them.

chapters.title: Turning toward pleasantness
chapters.description: Rob points to the broader emotional field of practice and suggests leaning toward what is okay, pleasant, or nourishing instead of only what feels wrong.

chapters.title: Meeting sitting pain
chapters.description: He offers several ways to work with discomfort, including whole-body awareness, resting attention elsewhere, and imagining the breath into the painful area.

chapters.title: Posture and settling
chapters.description: The guided section begins with finding an upright, easeful posture, softening tension, and connecting with the felt sense of the body.

chapters.title: Guided breath practice
chapters.description: He leads the meditation through lengthening the breath, then settling into a breath that is most comfortable and enjoyable while keeping awareness spacious.

chapters.title: Influences and recitations
chapters.description: The later section contains a long string of names, with recurring references to teachers and writers, especially Rainer Maria Rilke, though the surrounding context is incomplete.

title: First Samatha Instructions and Guided Meditation on the Breath
speaker: Rob Burbea
podcast_description: Rob Burbea offers first instructions for samatha practice by placing breath meditation inside a larger Buddhist frame: cultivation on one side, insight on the other. He then guides a long practice that emphasizes patience, non-judgment, kindness, and the quality of attention over counting or achievement. The meditation moves through posture, relaxing the body, lengthening the breath, and widening awareness so the breath can feel bright, comfortable, and alive.
short_summary: A teaching and guided breath meditation that stresses quality of attention, kindness, and calmness within the larger path of cultivation and insight.

chapters.title: The bigger picture
chapters.description: Burbea frames samatha as part of a balanced path of cultivation and insight, then briefly clarifies vipassana as clear seeing that leads to freedom.

chapters.title: Why practice with breath
chapters.description: He returns to the question of why the breath is being used at all, explaining that the breath supports calmness, brightness, and a mind fit for investigation.

chapters.title: Patience over self-judgment
chapters.description: The teaching turns to the repeated wandering of the mind as normal practice, inviting patience, non-judgment, and self-kindness instead of discouragement.

chapters.title: Quality, not counting
chapters.description: He distinguishes applied and sustained attention, then warns against turning meditation into a numbers game, urging vivid, momentary quality of attention.

chapters.title: Settling into the body
chapters.description: The guided meditation begins with posture, relaxing the face, throat, shoulders, and belly, then sensing the whole body and the breath in a spacious way.

chapters.title: Whole-body breathing
chapters.description: Attention is broadened to the body as a whole, with the in-breath felt as expansion and energizing and the out-breath as natural relaxation and letting go.

chapters.title: Adjusting the breath
chapters.description: He invites a more intimate exploration of where the breath is felt, then encourages finding the most comfortable and enjoyable way to breathe right now.

chapters.title: Bright and alive
chapters.description: In the closing minutes, the instruction is to keep the awareness bright, sensitive, and receptive to whatever the breath and body are doing.

title: Guided Meditation: Metta to phenomena
speaker: Rob Burbea
description: (Gaia House) This series of talks and guided meditations explores the development of the practices of both Lovingkindness and Compassion, with particular emphasis on the radical possibilities of Awakening that they bring. Through these practices we come to develop deep and beautiful qualities of heart as a real resource both for ourselves and the world, and also open ourselves to the profound and liberating understandings that can emerge from this path of love.
podcast_description: This guided meditation turns lovingkindness and compassion toward experience itself rather than toward people. Rob Burbea invites repeated softening, bathing whatever is present in kindness, and then experiments with a more receptive mode of complete welcoming. The emphasis gradually shifts away from precision and analysis toward acceptance, including resistance, numbness, fear, anger, and tiredness.
short_summary: A guided metta practice directed toward phenomena in awareness, moving from gentle bathing to complete receptive acceptance.

chapters.title: Turning Toward Experience
chapters.description: The meditation begins by redirecting metta and compassion from beings to whatever is prominent in awareness.

chapters.title: Bathing the Moment
chapters.description: He encourages repeatedly bathing body sensations, emotions, thoughts, and mind states in kindness and love.

chapters.title: Holding with Metta
chapters.description: The practice continues with phrases and a steady, tender holding of whatever is arising in experience.

chapters.title: Open Reception
chapters.description: Burbea introduces a more receptive mode of complete welcoming and an open door to whatever appears.

chapters.title: Softening Repeatedly
chapters.description: The instruction settles into repeated softening, welcoming, and bathing the moment in warmth.

chapters.title: Less Precision, More Kindness
chapters.description: He asks listeners to let go of the usual emphasis on precise mindfulness and allow the edges of experience to blur if they do.

chapters.title: Welcoming All States
chapters.description: Even resistance, numbness, anger, fear, and tiredness are included in the scope of kindness.

chapters.title: Long Receptive Silence
chapters.description: The rest of the sit unfolds in extended silence, with the inquiry into relationship and experience continuing underneath the meditation.
